embeds.title: Roleplay in Server Channels
embeds.description: Roleplay involving your characters here should be carried out on the server in one of the many channels provided for that purpose, rather than in DMs. We put a lot of effort into the server, you might as well use it if you're here. RPP (and, in many cases, Experience) is awarded for scenes done in-server.

fields.name: 🗺 Map
fields.value: You can use the <#738987459651829830> channel to navigate between different RP areas throughout the city. The map will allow you to have three such areas open simultaneously. Every area in the map is represented by an in-character location in the Demiplane, contained in a channel category.

fields.name: 🚪 Navigation
fields.value: Every roleplay location category has a channel at the top marked with a 🚪 icon. This channel includes a series of reference images to help visualize the individual locations contained within that area, as well as a react menu (similar to the map) that will allow you to navigate between connected locations.

fields.name: 🧵 RP Threads
fields.value: Also contained in each navigation channel is a button to Join or Create  an RP thread. Each RP channel can have up to 5 threads to provide more places for people to RP. These threads are subject to the same guidelines as other channels outlined here.

embeds.title: Don't Interrupt Scenes
embeds.description: Be polite and respect your fellow players by not joining other people's RP without their explicit consent, and do not close someone else's scene without their permission (with the exception of Dead Scenes - see below).

fields.name: 🌐 Open RP Areas
fields.value:
There are a few `Open RP` rooms scattered throughout the city, which may be joined by any character at any time, even if others are already roleplaying there. They can be identified in the list by channel names including the 🌐 icon.
__*Please note*__ that the intent behind open RP channels is for people to be able to **join** ongoing scenes, and **not** to start additional scenes in the same channel. If you do not want others to join your scene, __do not use an Open RP channel__.

fields.name: Activity Tracker
fields.value: You can use the <#1099439209284116531> channel to see the current status of all RP channels, organized by area.

fields.name: 🔐 Private Channels
fields.value:
Certain channels are marked with a 🔐 icon. These are private channels, purchased via RPP, and are meant for exclusive use by the owner(s) and may not be used without the owners' express permission.
Those few channels marked with the 🌐 Open RP icon are exceptions to this, and may be treated as an Open RP Area.

embeds.title: Multiple Scenes
embeds.description: A character is not considered RP-locked, and may be in more than one scene simultaneously. We don't explicitly cap the number of simultaneous scenes you can be in, but RP locations ARE limited, so try not to take up too many channels or lock them for too long without replies. Channels which go for too long without replies might be considered dead, and the scenes ended. See below.

fields.name: Scene Status
fields.value: The server's bot has a built-in scene status tracker which can be accessed using the `/scenestatus` command. You can see a list of all open scenes you are currently participating in, and easily travel between their locations. Note that this only tracks scenes with new RP posts so, if any are missing, you simply need to make a post in the scene channel. Ending the scene will remove it from the active scene tracker.

embeds.title: Dead Scenes
embeds.description:
While you are free to end your own scenes at any time and for any reason, please be respectful of other players and don't close other people's scenes unless they meet certain criteria. If a scene has gone for an extended period of time without a response from either party, the channel may be free to use. Use the following as a guideline.
NOTE: If taking over a channel from a stale scene, you MUST first end the previous scene with the `/scene` command. If the bot needs help ending the scene, and you aren't sure, ask a <@&694285067723210843> for help.

fields.name: :no_entry: < 3 Days
fields.value: Less than three days: Channel is still being actively used

fields.name: :question: 3-7 Days
fields.value: Last reply was 3-7 days ago: Ask participants if they are still using it. Do not take the channel unless given permission.

fields.name: :green_circle: 7+ Days
fields.value: Last reply was more than 7 days ago: It's still polite to ask, but is not stricly necessary. If you take the channel, be sure to close the previous scene properly.

fields.name: :skull: 14+ Days
fields.value: Last reply was more than 14 days ago: This scene is dead, and there is no longer any need to ask. If you take the channel, be sure to close the previous scene properly.

embeds.title: Ending Scenes
embeds.description: The server has a bot that will close scenes and award experience to participants. To close a scene, input the `/scene` command. The bot will attempt to find a best-match for each character, but may ask if it isn't sure. If the bot needs help ending the scene, and you aren't sure, ask a <@&694285067723210843> for help.

fields.name: <:xp:858887927899226112> Experience Awarded
fields.value: Once the scene is concluded, it will be sent to the DMs for approval (the exp award is not automatic). Please be patient, as it may take some time for a DM to review it. Once it has been reviewed and approved, the amount of exp awarded will be posted in <#775545148192587796>. Note that the Exp is **not** automatically added. You will need to run the `!xp <amt>` command in <#704307298407022622>, where `<amt>` is the exp award listed in the log channel.

The amount of exp awarded is calculated based on the duration of the RP, the amount of RP each player put into the scene, and the level of the character involved, and is capped based on the number of days the scene was ongoing. Please note that a player is not guaranteed to receive the full cap amount for a given scene.
